gaps.reason: `use core::fmt` — `core` is not a sibling module OR sibling phylum transpiled in this same batch (an external crate, `std`, or simply out of this batch's target set). Flagged, not guessed (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Marker super-trait: *this value is an honest stdlib error*."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A type is an [`StdError`] iff it is `Debug + Display + std::error::Error` — i.e. it can be"] # [doc = " shown to a human (`Display`), inspected by a developer (`Debug`), and chained as a cause"] # [doc = " (`std::error::Error::source`). The blanket impl below means **every** type already meeting"] # [doc = " that bound *is* an `StdError` with no per-type opt-in; the trait exists to give the"] # [doc = " contract a *name* the rest of the library (and tests) can bound on:"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_core::error_scaffold::StdError;"] # [doc = " fn surface<E: StdError>(e: &E) -> String { e.to_string() }"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Contract named (not enforced-by-types — RFC-0016 §4.1)"] # [doc = " - **C1 / G2 (never-silent).** An `StdError` is a *value*: producing one is the explicit,"] # [doc = "   non-sentinel signal that an operation refused. The scaffold cannot enforce that a crate"] # [doc = "   *returns* it rather than clamping — that is each op's obligation — but it gives the"] # [doc = "   refusal a uniform, honest type."] # [doc = " - **C3 (EXPLAIN / no black box).** `Display` carries the human-legible *why*; `source()`"] # [doc = "   carries the machine-walkable cause chain. An `Err` is as inspectable as an `Ok`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (a marker with a trivial blanket impl — no behaviour of its own)."] pub trait StdError : fmt :: Debug + fmt :: Display + std :: error :: Error { }
gaps.reason: trait `StdError` has supertrait bound(s) — trait_item grammar has no supertrait syntax (`'trait' Ident type_params? '{' ...`)

gaps.snippet: impl < T : ? Sized + fmt :: Debug + fmt :: Display + std :: error :: Error > StdError for T { }
gaps.reason: impl-level generic parameter(s) on a *trait-instance* impl (`impl<..> Trait for ..`) have no confirmed mapping yet — DN-130 (parametric trait-instance heads + coherence) is the unbuilt scope for that case; DN-131 authorizes only the inherent-impl slot

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Emit the mechanical `impl std::error::Error` marker for an error type, optionally with a"] # [doc = " `source()` delegate — and **nothing else**."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the boilerplate every `mycelium-std-*/src/error.rs` repeats verbatim. The caller"] # [doc = " keeps its own `#[derive(Debug, …)]` and its own hand-written `impl Display` (the"] # [doc = " domain-specific message is *intentionally* not automated — DN-17 §3/§5); the macro only"] # [doc = " removes the empty / mechanically-identical `impl std::error::Error`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Forms"] # [doc = " ```text"] # [doc = " impl_std_error!(MyError);                        // -> impl std::error::Error for MyError {}"] # [doc = " impl_std_error!(MyError<T>, generics = [T: Debug], where = [T: Debug]);  // generic"] # [doc = " impl_std_error!(MyError, source = |this| { match this { … } });          // source() delegate"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `source = |this| …` arm receives `&self` as `this` and must return"] # [doc = " `Option<&(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)>` — exactly the body the caller would write."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Why a macro and not a `#[derive]`"] # [doc = " KC-3 / KISS: a `proc-macro` would add a build-time dependency and a separate crate for a"] # [doc = " two-line impl. A `macro_rules!` keeps the dependency floor at zero and the expansion"] # [doc = " trivially auditable (`cargo expand` shows the exact impl). It is **opt-in**: a crate may"] # [doc = " keep writing the impl by hand and nothing breaks."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` — expands to the precise impl the caller would otherwise write."] # [macro_export] macro_rules ! impl_std_error { ($ ty : ty) => { impl :: std :: error :: Error for $ ty { } } ; ($ ty : ty , generics = [$ ($ g : tt) *] $ (, where = [$ ($ w : tt) *]) ? $ (,) ?) => { impl <$ ($ g) *> :: std :: error :: Error for $ ty $ (where $ ($ w) *) ? { } } ; ($ ty : ty , source = |$ this : ident | $ body : block $ (,) ?) => { impl :: std :: error :: Error for $ ty { fn source (& self) -> :: core :: option :: Option <& (dyn :: std :: error :: Error + 'static) > { let $ this = self ; $ body } } } ; }
gaps.reason: `macro_rules!` definition — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Compile-and-run assertion that `e` satisfies the `std::error::Error` (and thus [`StdError`])"] # [doc = " contract — the consolidation of the `*_is_std_error` test every error file repeats."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " It coerces `e` to `&dyn std::error::Error` (the compile-time half, exactly the existing"] # [doc = " `let _: &dyn std::error::Error = &e;` line) and exercises `Display` + `Debug` once (the"] # [doc = " run-time half — that neither panics and `Display` is non-empty), then returns the rendered"] # [doc = " `Display` string so a caller can fold the message check in the same call."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = " # #[cfg(any(test, feature = \"test-support\"))]"] # [doc = " # {"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_core::error_scaffold::assert_is_std_error;"] # [doc = " #[derive(Debug)]"] # [doc = " struct E;"] # [doc = " impl std::fmt::Display for E {"] # [doc = "     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result { f.write_str(\"boom\") }"] # [doc = " }"] # [doc = " impl std::error::Error for E {}"] # [doc = " let shown = assert_is_std_error(&E);"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(shown, \"boom\");"] # [doc = " # }"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` — a total predicate over a borrowed error; no approximation."] # [cfg (any (test , feature = "test-support"))] pub fn assert_is_std_error < E : StdError > (e : & E) -> String { let dynamic : & dyn std :: error :: Error = e ; let _ = format ! ("{dynamic:?}") ; let shown = dynamic . to_string () ; assert ! (! shown . is_empty () , "an StdError must render a non-empty Display message (C3 / EXPLAIN): {dynamic:?}") ; shown }
gaps.reason: type parameter `E` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Assert that an error's `Display` contains every `needle` — the consolidation of the"] # [doc = " near-identical `*_display_includes_*_fields` guard tests (each currently a hand-written"] # [doc = " run of `assert!(s.contains(..))`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Panics with a message naming the missing needle and the full rendered error, so a failing"] # [doc = " guard points straight at the dropped field."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = " # #[cfg(any(test, feature = \"test-support\"))]"] # [doc = " # {"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_core::error_scaffold::assert_display_contains;"] # [doc = " #[derive(Debug)]"] # [doc = " struct E { index: usize, len: usize }"] # [doc = " impl std::fmt::Display for E {"] # [doc = "     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {"] # [doc = "         write!(f, \"oob index={} len={}\", self.index, self.len)"] # [doc = "     }"] # [doc = " }"] # [doc = " impl std::error::Error for E {}"] # [doc = " assert_display_contains(&E { index: 5, len: 3 }, &[\"5\", \"3\"]);"] # [doc = " # }"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` — a total substring predicate; no approximation."] # [cfg (any (test , feature = "test-support"))] pub fn assert_display_contains < E : StdError > (e : & E , needles : & [& str]) { let shown = e . to_string () ; for needle in needles { assert ! (shown . contains (needle) , "Display of {e:?} must contain {needle:?} (field would be silently dropped); \
             got {shown:?}") ; } }
gaps.reason: type parameter `E` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] enum SampleErr { IndexOob { index : usize , len : usize } , Refused { why : String } , } impl fmt :: Display for SampleErr { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { SampleErr :: IndexOob { index , len } => { write ! (f , "index out of bounds (index={index}, len={len})") } SampleErr :: Refused { why } => write ! (f , "refused: {why}") , } } } impl_std_error ! (SampleErr) ; # [derive (Debug)] struct Wrapper { inner : SampleErr , } impl fmt :: Display for Wrapper { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "wrapper: {}" , self . inner) } } impl_std_error ! (Wrapper , source = | this | { Some (& this . inner) }) ; # [derive (Debug)] struct Narrowed < T : fmt :: Debug > { rejected : T , } impl < T : fmt :: Debug > fmt :: Display for Narrowed < T > { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "not representable: {:?}" , self . rejected) } } impl_std_error ! (Narrowed < T >, generics = [T : fmt :: Debug] , where = [T : fmt :: Debug]) ; # [doc = " The blanket impl makes any `Debug + Display + Error` type an `StdError` — no opt-in."] # [doc = " Guard: removing the blanket impl makes this fail to compile."] # [test] fn blanket_impl_covers_conforming_types () { fn require_std_error < E : StdError > (_ : & E) { } require_std_error (& SampleErr :: IndexOob { index : 1 , len : 0 }) ; require_std_error (& Wrapper { inner : SampleErr :: Refused { why : "x" . into () } , }) ; require_std_error (& Narrowed { rejected : 7u8 }) ; } # [doc = " The plain macro form yields a working `std::error::Error` whose `source()` is `None`."] # [doc = " Guard: a macro that emitted a non-trivial `source()` here would fail."] # [test] fn plain_macro_yields_std_error_without_source () { let e = SampleErr :: Refused { why : "consumed" . into () , } ; let dynamic : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; assert ! (std :: error :: Error :: source (dynamic) . is_none () , "plain impl_std_error! must not invent a source()") ; } # [doc = " The `source =` macro form chains to the inner error (the io/recover pattern)."] # [doc = " Guard: dropping the source arm makes `source()` return None and this fails."] # [test] fn source_macro_form_chains_cause () { let w = Wrapper { inner : SampleErr :: IndexOob { index : 9 , len : 3 } , } ; let src = std :: error :: Error :: source (& w) . expect ("source() must chain to inner") ; assert ! (src . to_string () . contains ("index out of bounds") , "source() must expose the inner error's Display; got {src}") ; } # [doc = " `assert_is_std_error` returns the rendered Display and rejects an empty message."] # [test] fn assert_is_std_error_returns_display () { let shown = assert_is_std_error (& SampleErr :: IndexOob { index : 5 , len : 3 }) ; assert ! (shown . contains ("index out of bounds")) ; } # [doc = " `assert_display_contains` passes when all needles are present."] # [test] fn assert_display_contains_accepts_present_needles () { assert_display_contains (& SampleErr :: IndexOob { index : 5 , len : 3 } , & ["index out of bounds" , "5" , "3"] ,) ; } # [doc = " `assert_display_contains` panics when a needle is missing (a dropped field)."] # [doc = " Guard: this is the failure mode the helper exists to catch."] # [test] # [should_panic (expected = "must contain")] fn assert_display_contains_rejects_missing_needle () { assert_display_contains (& SampleErr :: Refused { why : "x" . into () } , & ["NOT-PRESENT"]) ; } # [doc = " The macro does not alter the hand-written Display message in any way (behaviour-"] # [doc = " preserving — the whole point). Guard: any message rewrite by the macro fails here."] # [test] fn macro_preserves_handwritten_display_verbatim () { let e = SampleErr :: IndexOob { index : 2 , len : 1 } ; assert_eq ! (e . to_string () , "index out of bounds (index=2, len=1)") ; } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
